---
format: revealjs
editor: visual
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---


```{r}
#|include: false
library(tidyverse)
library(gifski)
library(av)
```


## {.center}
<h3 class>[R Tip of the Day]{.orange}</h3>
<h2 class="r-fit-text">Interactive Data Exploration</h2>
<h2 class="r-fit-text">with [GWalkR]{.flow}</h2>

![](https://github.com/Kanaries/GWalkR/blob/main/docs/img/hex_logo.png?raw=true)

::: footer
<span style="color:#94a3b8;">Yareli Rosales • SDS 264 • Spring 2026</span>
:::


## What is GWalkR? {auto-animate=true}

. . .

> `GWalkR` is an interactive R package for Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) that allows users to visualize and explore data using a drag-and-drop interface.

. . .

* It turns your data frame into a Tableau-style dashboard directly inside R.

. . .

* Using `GWalkR` makes it faster to understand patterns, relationships, and distributions in your data.

. . .

* It is especially useful at the beginning of a data analysis workflow.



## Why use it?

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
- Explore data without writing complex code
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
- Build visualizations using drag-and-drop
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
- Quickly understand patterns, distributions, and relationships
:::


## How to use GWalkR {auto-animate=true .smaller}

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
1. Install and load the package

```{.r code-line-numbers="1"}
install.packages("GWalkR")
library(GWalkR)
```
:::



::: {.fragment .fade-left}
```{r}
#|echo: false
library(GWalkR)
library(tidyverse)
```
:::


::: {.fragment .fade-in}
2. Pass your dataset into `gwalkr()`
```{.r code-line-numbers="2"}
gwalkr(iris)
```
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-left}
```{r}
#|echo: false
gwalkr(iris)
```
